lens: Cut complexity to the load-bearing minimum; reject machinery built for problems you don't have yet; demand observability on any swallowed error path.
round1Position: Core is sound and the cut→POV reflow is genuinely load-bearing — but two things blocked me. (B4) The admin Overview was a ~12-section wall: the cockpit opened onto stacked lenses instead of the decision. (B3) The trust ladder shipped real auto-send machinery for a problem that doesn't exist yet (no client has earned 3 cycles), AND the auto-send fired in the same tick as the operator doorbell inside a swallowed try/catch — an unreviewed move could reach a client on a timer and a failed/zero batch could vanish silently with no log. I asked to cut ClientRunningOrder + one lens, consolidate the duplicated predicates, and lower the content floor 3→2.
round2Position: Both my blockers are genuinely resolved in the code, not just in the digest — I verified against the actual files on the-issue/phase-3-pushed-cron. B4: the admin overview is now a real 4-5 beat spine (IssueHeader → StanceBar → DraftedPovEditor → BackingMovesQueue) with literally everything else collapsed into one closed <details>; ClientRunningOrder is not imported, KeywordTargetsLens is replaced by a single deep-link ClickableRow, and the disclosure children (ContentWorkOrderLens, TrustLadderPanel, StrategyDiff, cannibalization) each self-null when empty. A cold workspace opens to a decision, not a wall. B3: auto-send is dark-launched behind an OFF-by-default child flag that gates BOTH the cron invocation (line 214) and the operator UI (panel returns null), and — critically — the exact silent-vanish path I flagged now emits log.warn with candidateCount on the enabled-but-zero case, the week is stamped before the doorbell so the swallow is now correct rather than dangerous, and per-rec failures each warn. My cross-cutting asks also landed: predicates are now a single shared module (zero inline client re-impl), content floor is exactly 2 states, fabricated competitor strings are gone. I'm moved from block to ship.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: #4 — admin Overview was a ~12-section wall; cockpit opened onto stacked lenses instead of the decision
blockerVerdicts.note: Verified KeywordStrategy.tsx:580-699. issueOverviewEl renders a flat space-y-8 spine: IssueHeader → StanceBar → DraftedPovEditor → (conditional staleness nudge) → Add-rec button → BackingMovesQueue, then ONE <details> 'Supporting detail' closed by default holding everything else. ClientRunningOrder grep returns only the 'cut from v1' comment, never an import/render. KeywordTargetsLens replaced by a single ClickableRow deep-linking to seo-keywords (line 661). Confirmed the disclosure children self-null: ContentWorkOrderLens (line 91 'orders.length===0 → return null'), TrustLadderPanel (line 100-102), StrategyDiff (lines 29/37 'return null' on no-data/no-changes). orientEl does NOT self-null at this level, but it lives inside the collapsed details so it's irrelevant to the wall complaint. The cockpit opens to the decision. This is the cut I asked for.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: #3 — trust ladder = premature machinery + unreviewed auto-send on a timer inside a swallowed try/catch with no observability
blockerVerdicts.note: Verified strategy-issue-cron.ts and TrustLadderPanel.tsx. (a) Same OFF-by-default child flag strategy-trust-ladder-autosend gates BOTH the cron leg (line 214 — runAutoSendForWorkspace never called when off; doorbell+push stand) AND the panel (line 100 'if (!autoSendEnabled) return null', hooks called unconditionally first — Rules of Hooks honored, hidden panel makes zero network calls via the enabled arg). (b) My specific swallowed-error worry is now defensible: week is stamped BEFORE the doorbell (line 181, explicit comment), the doorbell swallow is justified because the visible bell derives from the stamp not the activity row, and the enabled-but-zero batch — the exact silent-vanish path I flagged — emits log.warn with candidateCount at line 302; per-rec failures warn at line 274. The YAGNI concern is conceded honestly: the machinery still exists and the same-tick coupling is dormant not removed, but dark-launched-off is the correct launch posture and the removalCondition explicitly requires a decoupled-tick + operator-veto window before any enable.

residualConcerns: The same-tick coupling is dormant, not removed. The day someone flips strategy-trust-ladder-autosend on per-workspace, an unreviewed move STILL reaches a client in the same cron tick that rings the doorbell — there is no veto/review window in the code, only a flag in front of it. The removalCondition documents this correctly, but a flag is a softer guarantee than an architecture. When this gets enabled, re-audit that the decoupled-tick path actually shipped first and isn't quietly skipped under deadline pressure.

Whole runAutoSendForWorkspace function (68 lines, sendRecommendation→markRecommendationAutoSent→mirror, getEarnedEnabledArchetypes, the 3-consecutive-cycle earning ladder, the autosend policy table/hook/UI) is live code maintained for a feature zero customers can trigger today (no workspace has earned 3 cycles). It's correctly inert, but it's still surface area that every future refactor of the send spine has to keep green. Acceptable for launch; revisit if it's still dark 2 quarters out — that's the YAGNI bill coming due.

orientEl inside the Supporting-detail disclosure is <OrientZone> rendered whenever isRealStrategy — it does not self-null at the call site like its siblings do. It's collapsed so it doesn't hurt the wall, but if OrientZone renders empty chrome it's a minor inconsistency with the 'everything self-nulls' claim. Low severity, worth a one-line check.
newConcerns: The B4 'zero empty cards on a cold workspace' guarantee now depends on a chain of independent self-null checks across five separate components (ContentWorkOrderLens, TrustLadderPanel, StrategyDiff, cannibalization leaf, orient). Each is correct today, but there's no single test asserting 'cold workspace → disclosure body renders nothing.' A future component added to that <details> without a self-null guard silently reintroduces a (collapsed) wall and nothing catches it. Cheap to add a render test.
killerQuestion: When strategy-trust-ladder-autosend is eventually flipped on for a real workspace, what in the CODE — not the flag's removalCondition prose — forces the decoupled-tick + operator-veto window to exist before the first auto-send fires? Right now the answer appears to be 'nothing but operator discipline,' and the same-tick send path is still wired and ready.

lens: Senior admin/operator UX — the curation cockpit: information density, send-model clarity, operator-facing affordance precedence
round1Position: Curation loop is genuinely fast, BUT: ~13 co-equal sections including empty placeholder cards (a wall, not a decision surface); THREE competing send affordances (per-row send, bulk send, header send) with no stated precedence; disabled "Send issue" gives no reason; StanceBar and queue archetype colors were swapped (authority_bet teal in one, blue in the other).
round2Position: Both owned blockers are genuinely resolved in the shipped code, not just the digest. The wall is cut to a verified 4-beat spine (IssueHeader → StanceBar → DraftedPovEditor → BackingMovesQueue) plus one closed-by-default "Supporting detail" disclosure; KeywordTargetsLens file still exists but has zero JSX call sites (confirmed by grep), ClientRunningOrder is gone, and empty lenses self-null so a cold cockpit shows no placeholder SectionCards. The three competing sends collapse to ONE canonical model: CockpitRow's primary button, when onStage is passed (which it is in the Issue path), toggles a local useToggleSet with aria-pressed and provably never calls actions.send or setMode('send'); the header "Send issue" is the sole client commit. Both my round-1 nits are also fixed: disabled Send now shows a visible, aria-describedby-wired reason string, and StanceBar + queue both key off the shared ARCHETYPE_ACCENT map so the teal/blue swap is gone. I'd ship this.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Blocker #4 — wall of ~13 co-equal sections incl. empty placeholder cards
blockerVerdicts.note: Verified in src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:580-699: the issueOverviewEl renders a 4-beat spine (IssueHeader, StanceBar, DraftedPovEditor, BackingMovesQueue) + a single closed-by-default <details> 'Supporting detail'. grep confirms KeywordTargetsLens has ZERO JSX render sites (only its own file's self-references and two comments) — it's replaced by one ClickableRow deep-link (line 661). ClientRunningOrder is fully gone (not imported anywhere). Empty lenses self-null (ContentWorkOrderLens/TrustLadderPanel/cannibalization), and BackingMovesQueue's empty branch (line 230, gated on groups.length===0) shows a single 'No recommendations to back the issue yet.' line, not a placeholder card. A cold cockpit genuinely shows no empty SectionCard chrome. This is the real fix, not a fixture-masked one.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Blocker #5 — three competing send affordances with no stated precedence
blockerVerdicts.note: This is the load-bearing claim and it holds in CockpitRow.tsx:139-155. When onStage is provided (the Issue path always passes it via BackingMovesQueue→CockpitRow), the primary button renders variant primary/secondary, sets aria-pressed={staged}, and onClick={() => onStage(rec.id)} — it does NOT call setMode('send') or actions.send. Only the non-Issue legacy path (the else branch) opens the send panel. Staging is a local useToggleSet<string> (KeywordStrategy.tsx:190); stageMany is a set UNION (line 527); NO client write occurs on stage. The header handleSendIssue (line 529) is the ONE commit, firing issueBulkSend.mutate({recIds: stagedSendableIds, action:'send'}) then clearing the set. Precedence is now unambiguous: rows stage, header sends. The counter '{stagedCount} staged · {curatedCount} already with client' is surfaced in both IssueHeader and BackingMovesQueue, and numerator/denominator share one cockpitRecs source via the shared isCuratedForClient predicate.

residualConcerns: KeywordTargetsLens.tsx remains in the tree with zero call sites — dead code, not a launch blocker, but it's exactly the kind of orphan that gets accidentally re-wired by a future agent who greps the issue/ dir and assumes a component is meant to render. Should be deleted (or have a render-removed comment) rather than left dormant.

Operator discoverability of the stage→send model is untested with real users. The header counter and the 'Stage for issue / Staged' toggle are correct, but an operator who has used the legacy per-row 'Send to client' flow may stage a batch and not realize nothing reached the client until they hit 'Send issue'. The disabled-reason copy ('Stage moves below to send.') mitigates this, but only when stagedCount===0 — once one rec is staged the button enables and the 'you still must commit' signal goes quiet.

Fast-follows explicitly out of launch scope that I'd want on the immediate roadmap, not deferred indefinitely: portfolio triage (#7) and the outcome/export one-pager (#10). Neither blocks launch, but an operator running many workspaces will feel the absence of triage quickly.
newConcerns: The staged set is local React state (useToggleSet), so it is wiped on tab unmount / navigation away / refresh. An operator who stages a 10-rec batch, gets distracted, and navigates away loses the staging with no warning. Low severity (re-staging is cheap and the data is non-destructive), but it's a new ephemeral-state footgun the previous per-row-send model didn't have, since each send was its own committed action.

'Supporting detail' is one collapsed disclosure holding 6+ heterogeneous lenses (keyword targets row, ContentWorkOrderLens, TrustLadderPanel, orient, cannibalization, strategyDiff, competitor link). The anti-wall fix succeeds for the cold case, but for a mature workspace where all of those self-null TRUE, opening that single <details> re-creates a mini-wall inside the disclosure with no internal hierarchy. The wall was relocated, not eliminated, for the data-rich case.
killerQuestion: If an operator stages eight moves and then navigates away (or the tab unmounts) before clicking "Send issue", the local useToggleSet is silently discarded with no unsaved-state warning — what protects the operator from believing they sent an issue they only ever staged?

persona: Skeptical owner of a small local service business (plumbing/HVAC) — non-technical, time-poor, watches every dollar, burned by an agency before
round1Position: Rated "matters: somewhat." Liked the plain-English status line and proof-first framing, but had one HARD blocker: "Act on this" showed no price, no scope, no tier gate, and the toast said "we'll scope it and get to work" — reading like I'd just authorized a charge, so my hand froze over the button. Two softer concerns: evergreen/no-dates removed my reason to return weekly, and content-leading smelled like the agency pushing high-margin blog work over the near-me rankings that actually book my jobs.
round2Position: My hard blocker is genuinely dead. I verified it in the code, not just the digest: the button no longer says "Act on this" — it says "Request this" (or "Discuss this" for non-money moves), clicking it opens a confirm dialog that says in plain words "Nothing is billed at this click," Cancel writes nothing, and the success toast says "your strategist will scope and confirm before any work or charge." More importantly the server backs that up — a Free client on a paid-type move gets a hard 403 BEFORE anything is written, and there is zero Stripe/charge code anywhere in that route. So the ambiguity that made me freeze is gone: I now know pressing it does not invoice me. That alone moves me off "frozen hand." But it does NOT move me to "yes, decisive" — because the two things that decide whether I keep paying are untouched. Content still LEADS as the hero; my near-me/local rankings are still a single de-emphasized "Local visibility — Getting found in your service area" count-row buried under the content cards (confirmed in IssueAlsoOnPlanSection). And there's still no reason to open this weekly — no "since you last looked" hook was added, evergreen is unchanged. So: hard blocker gone, but I'm still "somewhat." Safer to click, not more reason to stay.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Blocker #1 — "Act on this" pricing/scope/tier ambiguity (the frozen-hand button)
blockerVerdicts.note: Verified at source, not just the digest. CLIENT: IssueContentCard.tsx — the literal "Act on this" is gone (verb is "Request this"/"Discuss this"); a locked descriptor renders a <TierGate> upsell instead of an active button; every POST is preceded by a single ConfirmDialog whose message is the no-charge line ISSUE_REQUEST_CONFIRM_CONSEQUENCE = 'Your strategist will confirm scope before any work begins. Nothing is billed at this click.'; Cancel writes nothing; success toast = 'Added to your plan — your strategist will scope and confirm before any work or charge.' SERVER (the part that actually matters to a skeptic): recommendations.ts:532-538 returns res.status(403) {requiredTier:'growth'} for a Free client on a monetizable type BEFORE the db.transaction(), so a rejected request creates NOTHING — no greenlight, no work item. grep for stripe/charge/checkout/invoice in that whole route returns ZERO. And the registry (recommendation-lifecycle.ts) marks only content/content_refresh/schema/accessibility monetizable — local_visibility, local_service_gap, keyword_gap are all 'Discuss this' (a free conversation), which is exactly right for my kind of business. This is precisely the fix my round-1 killer question demanded: I can now tell whether a click charges me, and it doesn't.

residualConcerns: WEEKLY RETURN (round-1 HIGH, not my owned blocker, still open): no 'since you last looked' hook was added — grep for 'since you last'/'new this week' in the issue components returns nothing. Evergreen-no-dates is structurally unchanged, so a returning page looks identical to last Tuesday and I still assume nothing happened. The pricing fix makes the button safe to press; it does nothing to pull me back.

CONTENT-OVER-LOCAL (round-1 MEDIUM, still open): the resolution did NOT reorder for local-service clients. Content is still the hero; 'Local visibility — Getting found in your service area' is still ONE de-emphasized ClickableRow count in 'Also on your plan' (IssueAlsoOnPlanSection.tsx:32), below the content cards. The work that books my jobs is still subordinate to the blog work. The fact that local moves are non-monetizable ('Discuss this', free) softens the upsell smell a little, but the ordering still serves their margin, not my phone ringing.

SEE-THE-DETAILS DEAD-END (round-1 MEDIUM, still open): onSeeDetails still navigates to the full 'strategy' page root (openStrategy at TheIssueClientPage.tsx:191) — the cluttered wall this redesign was supposed to spare me. Curiosity still bounces me from the calm surface into the noise.

READ-MOSTLY (round-1 MEDIUM, still open): by month three the page is still six look-only sections and one real action. The retainer still risks feeling like a subscription to a dashboard rather than proof of work done. The fixes made the one action safer; they didn't add a recurring 'here's the work WE did this period' proof beat.
newConcerns: The 'Let us talk' soft-yes opens the chat advisor pre-seeded with the move (handleLetsTalk → onOpenChat + onAskAi) — genuinely good, it gives a burned owner a free 'just talk to me first' path beside the request verb. Minor nit, not a real worry: for a Free client on a paid content move, the active path is replaced by a TierGate upsell, and it's not 100% obvious the free 'Let us talk' conversation valve is still reachable in that locked state — if upgrading is the only visible next step on a locked card, that re-introduces a faint 'pay to even discuss it' feel. Worth a glance, not a blocker.
killerQuestion: The button is finally safe to press — but when I open this next Tuesday, what on the page tells me you actually did anything for me since last time, and why is 'am I showing up for emergency-plumber-near-me' still a one-line afterthought under another blog post you'd like me to pay for?

persona: B2B marketing manager who defends SEO budget upward to a VP/CFO
lens: Budget-defense / spend-justification: can I take this surface into a budget review and prove the retainer earns its keep — ideally in pipeline/revenue terms, exportable, period-over-period?
round1Position: "Matters: somewhat." Loved the ROI honesty (organic traffic value + ad-spend-equivalent, no fake revenue promises) and the human-curation moat. But two real gaps: (1) the surface tops out at traffic value — my VP wants pipeline/MQL/revenue; (2) no exportable one-pager and evergreen-no-dates fights the period-over-period delta I need to defend spend in a budget cycle. Plus the shared blocker I owned: "Act on this" had no price/scope/tier gate — clarity problem.
round2Position: My owned hard blocker (#1 clarity) is genuinely, verifiably resolved — this is a real fix, not a relabel. Server-authoritative 403 (requiredTier:'growth') fires BEFORE any db.transaction (recommendations.ts:532-538), so a Free client on a monetizable rec gets nothing written; the UI mirrors it with a locked TierGate that renders an upsell instead of an active button (IssueContentCard.tsx:183-194); the verb is "Request this"/"Discuss this" (the bare "Act on this" is gone); and there's a ConfirmDialog naming the rec + "Nothing is billed at this click" (evergreenCopy.ts:55-56). That kills the freeze I flagged. The proof re-sequence is also real (plan leads, ROI collapsed behind "See full report"). BUT my second gap is structurally unmoved and the team knows it: the proof ceiling is still "Organic traffic value" + "Ad spend equivalent," and the methodology disclosure explicitly says "We do not multiply by lead value, close rate, or lifetime value... not a promise of booked revenue" (ROIDashboard.tsx:68). The export/funnel one-pager — the actual budget-defense artifact — is deferred to FF3, and the plan candidly names it: "The B2B/CMO budget-defense ask — they won't expand without it" (tournament plan line 668). Evergreen also deliberately suppresses the period-over-period MoM stat on my surface (ROIDashboard.tsx:189 `&& !evergreen`); the period-over-period view is reserved for the future export. So: the hard blocker is gone, but the thing that would move ME from "somewhat" to "decisive" — exportable pipeline/revenue proof — was explicitly carved out of launch.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Blocker #1 — "Act on this" had no price, no scope, no tier gate (gave away the content SKU free; Free/Growth/Premium greenlit identically)
blockerVerdicts.note: Verified end-to-end, not just in the digest. Server gate: recommendations.ts:532-538 returns 403 + requiredTier:'growth' for Free+monetizable BEFORE the db.transaction() at line 550 — nothing is written, no greenlight, no content request, no Stripe I/O. The actOnDescriptor (line 136-145) is the matching UI projection and is correctly gated inside the exposeClientStatus/flag-ON path so it's absent when the flag is OFF. Client mirror: IssueContentCard.tsx:183-194 renders a TierGate upsell (with a bare <span/> child → no active button) for mode==='locked'; mode==='included' opens a single ConfirmDialog (lines 225-234) titled with the rec headline and messaged 'Your strategist will confirm scope before any work begins. Nothing is billed at this click.' Verb is 'Request this'/'Discuss this' — the literal 'Act on this' is gone. The clarity/freeze problem I owned is genuinely fixed.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Fast-follow #10 — outcome/export one-pager + conversions→organic funnel/pipeline bridge (my budget-defense artifact)
blockerVerdicts.note: Explicitly out of launch scope and honestly labeled as such — NOT silently dropped, which I credit. Tournament plan line 668 names it verbatim: 'Outcome/export one-pager (multi-day; new render/export path). The B2B/CMO budget-defense ask — they won't expand without it — but not a launch blocker.' Deferred to FF3 (line 153/452). The deferral carries a real guard that I verified shipped: the ROI methodology disclosure is preserved (ROIDashboard.tsx:163) and the stray MoM stat is reconciled — suppressed on the evergreen surface via `data.growthPercent != null && !evergreen` (line 189). So nothing regressed, but my core ask is still a promise, not a feature.

residualConcerns: Proof ceiling is unchanged for my use case: top line is still 'Organic traffic value' + 'Ad spend equivalent.' There IS a conditional 'Revenue at stake' tile (ROIDashboard.tsx:215-223) but it's a forward-looking 'value you'd unlock by moving keywords,' not attributed booked revenue or pipeline — and the methodology disclosure (line 68) correctly disclaims any close-rate/LTV math. I can't take this into a CFO review and say 'this drove $X pipeline.'

No export artifact at all — grep of src/components/client/the-issue/ for export/pdf/download/print returns zero. Budget defense happens in a deck or a doc I hand upward; a dashboard I have to screen-share live, with the proof collapsed behind a 'See full report' click, is the wrong format for that meeting.

Evergreen-no-dates cuts against me specifically. The re-sequence and dateless framing are right for the relationship/SMB client, but my budget cycle IS temporal — I need the period-over-period delta, and it was deliberately removed from my surface and reserved for an export that doesn't exist yet. The plan is internally consistent about this (line 687), but consistency doesn't give me the number.

'Request this' for Growth is request-only with no charge at launch — fine for trust, but it means the differentiated value-capture moment the surface implies is structural, not live. Doesn't affect MY budget-defense need, but it means the monetization story I'd cite upward is still partly aspirational.
newConcerns: The proof band is now ONE band collapsed behind a single 'See full report' details toggle (TheIssueClientPage.tsx:205-223). For the relationship client this is a clean improvement, but for the budget-defense segment it cuts the wrong way — collapsing all proof behind one click can read as 'the agency has less to show,' the opposite of what I want in a spend review. This is a known risk the tournament itself flagged (line 234). The numbers strip in the summary partly mitigates it, but the full ROI methodology — the part that makes the number defensible upward — is one click down.

Minor, not blocking: the 'Revenue at Stake' tile is gated on `data.revenueAtStake != null` and labeled with emerald/$ success styling. If a client's VP sees an emerald 'Revenue at Stake' dollar figure they may read it as booked revenue despite the methodology disclaimer being collapsed behind the reveal. The honest disclaimer exists but is one layer further from the eye-catching number than it was.
killerQuestion: You've correctly diagnosed that I 'won't expand without' the outcome/export one-pager and explicitly allowed it to be period-over-period — so what's the committed FF3 timeline, and until it ships, what exactly do I screen-share or hand to my CFO to defend this retainer at the next budget review, given the only exportable-feeling number is a disclaimed 'directional traffic-value model, not a promise of booked revenue'?

lens: Data-driven growth marketer who pays for SEO tooling and authorizes content spend — judges every dollar figure for falsifiability and every surface for whether it proves work moved a number.
round1Position: "Matters: somewhat." Genuinely better and unusually honest, but it leads with traffic-value theater instead of pipeline and can't answer "did the work move my number?" Praised the ROI disclaimer and one-decision-per-card; but the $ figures are clicks×CPC, vanity-adjacent without a since-engagement baseline or confidence range, it's thinner than the tools I already pay for, and the blurred fabricated competitor rows ("emergency service near me") are a trust scratch that makes me distrust everything else on the surface.
round2Position: Still "matters: somewhat" — but the hard trust blocker is gone and the honesty went UP, not down. My owned blocker (#6) is cleanly resolved: I read CompetitorGapsSection.tsx myself and the fabricated/blurred rows are eliminated — non-Premium fetches no data (gated on isPremium, line 58), server enforces a matching 402 (public-portal.ts:838), and what's blurred under TierGate is now one generic honest line ("Your strategist is mapping your competitive landscape"), not invented keyword strings. A repo grep confirms zero fabricated competitor strings survive in src/. That removes the credibility scratch that was poisoning my read of everything else — a real fix, cheaply done. The re-sequence (plan leads, proof collapsed behind "See full report") also directly addresses my "traffic-value theater first" complaint. BUT the thing that actually anchored me at "somewhat" is structurally untouched: the dollar model is still totalClicks × avgCPC (ROIDashboard.tsx:172), with no confidence range and no since-engagement baseline. The methodology disclosure is now even more candid — it explicitly disavows lead value/close rate/LTV and calls itself "a directional traffic-value model, not a promise of booked revenue" — which I respect, but candor about a vanity-adjacent number doesn't make it answer my question. My killer Q (greenlit moves → signups/revenue) maps exactly to fast-follow #10, which is explicitly out of launch scope. So: the blocker that made me distrust the product is resolved; the gap that kept me from "decisive" is deferred by design.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Blocker #6 — fabricated/blurred competitor teaser rows on a client trust surface
blockerVerdicts.note: Verified in src/components/client/CompetitorGapsSection.tsx directly. Non-Premium tiers do not fetch data (useClientCompetitorGaps gated on isPremium, line 58), so there is no real or fake competitor data client-side to blur. The TierGate child is a single honest generic line, 'Your strategist is mapping your competitive landscape.' (line 78) — what blurs is that line, not invented keywords. Server enforces the same gate with a 402 at public-portal.ts:838, so data never reaches non-Premium even if the UI is bypassed. Repo grep for 'near me'/'same-day repair'/'emergency service' in src/ returns only this file's own documenting comment and an unrelated BriefGenerator input placeholder — zero live fabricated strings. competitor-gaps-projection.ts shows no Math.random/mock/synth; Premium users see real provider-derived gap.* fields as banded labels, never money. This is the cheapest high-trust fix in the report and it landed clean.

residualConcerns: The dollar model is unchanged and still unfalsifiable in the way I flagged: organicTrafficValue = totalClicks × avgCPC (ROIDashboard.tsx:172), with no confidence range (no low/high band) and no since-engagement baseline. I cannot tell whether $X is up because of the work or because of seasonality. The disclosure got MORE honest, but honesty about a directional number isn't the same as a defensible number.

No outcome/attribution bridge — the surface still cannot answer 'of the moves I greenlit, how many became signups or revenue?'. That is fast-follow #10, explicitly out of launch scope. Without it the ROI band is ad-spend-equivalence theater, which is exactly what cheaper tools already give me.

Still thinner than the tools I pay for on the analytics axis. The differentiator is the curated POV + one-decision card, which I do credit — but a data-literate buyer comparing the proof band head-to-head with Semrush/Ahrefs sees a directional CPC estimate, not something net-new.
No executive export / one-pager for budget defense (also fast-follow #10). I can't take this into a board deck, which is where a CMO actually needs an SEO ROI artifact to earn its keep.
newConcerns: The new 'revenueAtStake' metric (ROIDashboard.tsx:215, gated on data.revenueAtStake != null) is the one place that escalates beyond pure CPC framing — I want to be sure its inputs aren't quietly reintroducing the same unfalsifiable multiply-by-assumptions risk under a more confident label. The disclosure disavows lead-value math, so if revenueAtStake is shown it must be sourced from real conversion data, not a re-skinned estimate. Worth a look but not a blocker on the evidence I have.
killerQuestion: You fixed the fabricated competitor rows and you made the ROI disclaimer more honest — both real, and I credit them. But the question that decides whether I expand spend is still unanswered on the surface: of the moves I greenlit last quarter, how many became signups, demos, or revenue — and if the honest answer is 'we don't track that yet,' why should I treat your dollar band as anything more than a prettier ad-spend-equivalence number I can already get from the tools I pay for?
round2Rating: matters: somewhat (hard trust blocker resolved; decisive-tier gap deferred by design)

lens: Holistic UI/UX — information hierarchy, decision affordances, accessibility, mobile touch targets, send-model coherence
round1Position: Architecture sound and color-law compliance excellent, but two structural failures: (B2) a hierarchy inversion buried the decision surface — ROI/numbers rendered above or co-equal to the content plan, so the money surface read second on mobile; and (B5) the send affordance was incoherent — per-row and bulk "send" lived in multiple places, and the header "Send issue" disabled with no visible reason. Plus a11y gaps I called out: StanceBar segment title-attrs sat on bare divs invisible to screen readers; content-card controls failed 44px touch targets.
round2Position: Both owned blockers are genuinely resolved in the shipped code, and the a11y sub-concerns I raised are fixed too — not papered over. B2: TheIssueClientPage.tsx renders the content plan as section 3 (HERO) ABOVE the proof band at section 5, with ROI collapsed inside a user-action <details> and the numbers strip moved into that summary — plan leads, proof follows, verified in source not just the digest. B5: the cockpit now has exactly one commit path — CockpitRow stages into a local useToggleSet (no client write, aria-pressed toggle), the bulk bar's 'send' action returns before any mutation and only stages, and the header "Send issue" is the sole commit via issueBulkSend.mutate, with stagedSendableIds reconciled against sendableRecIds so a stale staged rec can't be re-sent. My specific complaints are addressed: the disabled "Send issue" now carries a VISIBLE inline reason wired via aria-describedby ("Stage moves below to send." / "Everything curated is already with your client."), StanceBar segments now carry aria-label AND a text legend, and every content-card control has min-h-[44px] with a flex-col→sm:flex-row mobile stack. The staged/curated counter shares one cockpitRecs source for numerator and denominator. I rate this launch-ready on my lens.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: #2 Hierarchy inversion — decision/content surface buried below ROI on mobile
blockerVerdicts.note: Verified in TheIssueClientPage.tsx, not just the digest. Section order: (3) IssueContentPlanSection HERO at lines 179-193 renders ABOVE (5) the proof band at lines 200-224. The proof band is a single collapsed <details> whose summary holds the CompactStatBar numbers strip; ROIDashboard only mounts behind the 'See full report' user-action toggle. The header comment encodes the contract ('plan LEADS, proof FOLLOWS'). The old co-equal 'Your numbers' + ROI sections are merged into one band below the plan. On a phone the first money surface a client hits after the status headline is the content plan, which is exactly the inversion I flagged — now corrected. Content-floor fast-follow also landed: two honest states gated on hasContent (line 94 of IssueContentPlanSection.tsx), empty body renders the literal 'Your strategist is sizing up your next content opportunities.' (line 134), no 3-tier filler cards.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: #5 Send affordance — incoherent multi-place send + no disabled reason
blockerVerdicts.note: One canonical send model confirmed across all three layers. CockpitRow.tsx (lines 139-150): when onStage is threaded the primary button toggles staged-set membership with aria-pressed, label 'Stage for issue'/'Staged', and never calls actions.send or opens CockpitSendPanel. BackingMovesQueue.tsx (lines 272-278): the bulk bar's 'send' action stages the selection and RETURNS before any bulk.mutate — throttle/strike stay real mutations, but no client write on stage. KeywordStrategy.tsx orchestrator (lines 190, 529-536): stagedRecIds is a local useToggleSet; handleSendIssue is the lone commit firing issueBulkSend.mutate then clearing the set; stagedSendableIds reconciles against sendableRecIds (line 524) so a stale staged rec is dropped. My specific 'disables with no reason' complaint is fixed in IssueHeader.tsx (lines 63-104): an inline VISIBLE <span> reason ('Stage moves below to send.' vs 'Everything curated is already with your client.') wired to the button via aria-describedby — a screen-reader-announced reason, not a tooltip. The live 'N staged · M already with client' counter derives both numbers from one cockpitRecs set, satisfying the numerator/denominator-share-a-source rule.

residualConcerns: a11y win confirmed beyond my two blockers: StanceBar.tsx now gives each bar segment BOTH title and aria-label (lines 64-65) plus a real text legend below — but the segments are still bare non-interactive divs. Fine for decoration, but if a future iteration makes a segment clickable it must become a button with focus management; flag for whoever touches it next.

The content-card feedback buttons use px-2 py-1 with min-h-[44px] enforcing the vertical touch target, but the horizontal hit area is narrow (icon + short label). 44px tall is met; 44px wide is not guaranteed for 'Yes'/'No'-length labels. Minor, not a blocker, but worth a pass on small phones.

ROI behind a single <details> 'See full report' is the right call for hierarchy, but it now takes TWO taps (open band, then scroll) to reach the methodology disclosure that some clients use to trust the number. Acceptable tradeoff; monitor whether clients actually expand it.
Fast-follows explicitly out of launch scope (portfolio triage #7, external naming #8, Growth Stripe charge, outcome one-pager #10) are noted but not mine to gate — none block my lens.
killerQuestion: The staged set lives in component-local useToggleSet state, so if the operator stages eight moves and then navigates away or the cockpit remounts (a refetch, a tab switch, a flag flip), the staging is silently lost with no warning — was an in-flight 'you have N unsent staged moves' guard or persistence considered, or is the expectation that operators always stage-and-send in one uninterrupted sitting?
newConcerns: The Blocker-5 staged set is ephemeral component state (useToggleSet in KeywordStrategy.tsx, line 190) with no persistence or unsaved-work guard. A remount or navigation drops the staging silently — the fix that made staging local (correct for avoiding premature client writes) introduced a quiet data-loss-of-intent path that the per-row send model didn't have. Not a launch blocker, but a real UX regression hazard the old model didn't carry.

The digest mis-cited file paths (claimed KeywordStrategy.tsx at src/components/strategy/ lines 580-699; actual is src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx) and claimed KeywordTargetsLens was 'removed' when the file still exists (it is merely not rendered — confirmed no import, no JSX, only comments). The code is correct; the digest's provenance is loose. I verified against source rather than trust it, which is the only reason I can sign off.

lens: Monetization integrity — does every greenlight of a billable SKU pass through a tier/price gate, and is the unit economics honest to the client?
round1Position: Best-in-class retention surface, but the core monetization loop is a structural leak. "Act on this" minted an unpriced, uncharged content request (the $250-800 content SKU) with no tier gate; Free/Growth/Premium greenlit identically; and "Also on your plan" trained clients to expect billable work as already-included. Rated it a ship-blocker: you cannot launch a money surface that gives the money away.
round2Position: The leak is closed at the layer that matters — the server. I verified `server/routes/recommendations.ts:530-538`: before any DB write or transaction, a Free client on a monetizable archetype gets `403 {requiredTier:'growth'}` and nothing is minted. The gate is the route, not a hideable button; the `actOn` descriptor (line 218) is a faithful UI mirror, projected only behind the flag so flag-OFF is byte-identical. Verbs changed ("Request this"/"Discuss this", the bare "Act on this" is gone), a ConfirmDialog with an explicit no-charge consequence line precedes the POST, and "Also on your plan" was rebuilt as compact count-rows that EXCLUDE the content archetype — so the billable SKU is no longer co-located with "already included" framing. A dedicated integration test (`tests/integration/the-issue-acton-pricing.test.ts`) exercises all six cases I'd have demanded. I move from blocker to resolved-for-launch, with the deliberate caveat that the launch model is "tier-as-gate, no à-la-carte charge" — Growth/Premium clients greenlight unlimited monetizable moves at no incremental charge, which is a margin decision the owner made knowingly, not a leak.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Blocker #1 — monetization leak: unpriced/ungated content SKU greenlit identically across tiers
blockerVerdicts.note: Verified in code, not just the digest. server/routes/recommendations.ts:532-538 runs a server-authoritative gate BEFORE db.transaction(): `exposeClientStatus && effectiveTier==='free' && REC_POLICY_REGISTRY[type].monetizable` → 403 requiredTier:'growth', zero writes. REC_POLICY_REGISTRY (recommendation-lifecycle.ts:40-45) marks exactly content/content_refresh/schema/accessibility monetizable; keyword_gap/strategy/technical are false, so the gate neither under- nor over-fires. The actOn descriptor (line 218) is projected inside `if(exposeClientStatus)` so flag-OFF is byte-identical. Client mirror (IssueContentCard.tsx:182-194) renders a TierGate on `locked`, never an active button; included path gates the POST behind a ConfirmDialog whose message is 'Your strategist will confirm scope before any work begins. Nothing is billed at this click.' Integration test asserts Free+monetizable→403/no-request/clientStatus stays 'sent', Growth→one lineage-stamped request, Free+non-monetizable NOT gated, flag-OFF not gated, and both projection descriptors. This is the lock-step (route gate + UI mirror + test) I asked for in round 1. The leak is closed.

residualConcerns: TRIAL-WINDOW IDENTICAL-GREENLIGHT: computeEffectiveTier (server/workspaces.ts:59-66) promotes Free→Growth for the duration of an active trialEndsAt. So a 14-day-trial client greenlights monetizable moves identically to a paying Growth client, and the gate does not fire. This is intentional (the trial exists to demonstrate value) and is NOT the round-1 leak, but it is the one surviving slice of 'tiers greenlight identically' — a trial client can queue several content requests at zero charge, and there is no per-trial cap on how many billable moves they can stack before converting. Worth a deliberate owner decision, not a silent default.

NO MARGIN CONTROL ON GROWTH VOLUME: launch model is tier-as-gate with NO à-la-carte charge (priced mode exists in the union but is never emitted; no Stripe in the path). A Growth client can 'Request this' on every monetizable card every week. The plan bundles unlimited greenlights, so the unit cost of fulfilling them is unbounded against a flat MRR. The confirm copy ('your strategist will scope and confirm before any work or charge') is the only backstop, and it is a manual human gate, not a system limit. Acceptable at launch volume; becomes a margin problem at scale.

DEFERRED À-LA-CARTE (#fast-follow): the priced path is explicitly out of launch scope. Fine — but until it ships, the ONLY monetization lever on this surface is the Free→Growth upgrade. There is no way to monetize a one-off move from a client who doesn't want to change tiers, which is the exact warm-lead the 'Let us talk' soft-yes generates. The revenue capture for that valve is entirely manual/off-platform right now.
newConcerns: The success toast and confirm line both promise 'your strategist will scope and confirm before any work or charge.' That is a written commitment surfaced to the client at the moment of greenlight. If the fulfilment workflow ever auto-progresses a requested content item into billable work WITHOUT an operator scope/confirm step, the platform will have made a promise the operator can silently break. The promise is now load-bearing copy — it needs an operator-side gate that actually enforces 'confirm before charge,' or it becomes a chargeback/dispute liability. I did not verify that downstream gate exists.
killerQuestion: During an active trial a Free workspace resolves to effectiveTier 'growth' and the gate does not fire — what stops a trial client from stacking ten monetizable content requests in their 14 days and then letting the trial lapse, having captured ~$2,500-8,000 of queued billable scope at zero charge and zero tier commitment, and is there any per-trial greenlight cap or operator throttle, or is that volume entirely absorbed as trial-acquisition cost?

lens: Decision model coherence + warm-lead conversation surface (the soft-yes affordance a high-end firm lives on)
round1Position: Coherent "one curated set, four projections" spine — not a Frankenstein. BUT the client IA betrayed its own content-leads thesis (proof/numbers sat above the plan), and the decision model was two-headed: the admin cockpit let you send per-row AND in bulk AND from a header with no single canonical commit, while the client card had no in-card "let us talk" affordance — the soft-yes was a long scroll to the footer, suppressing exactly the warm-lead conversations a high-end firm runs on.
round2Position: Both blockers I owned are genuinely resolved in the code, not just the digest. B5: CockpitRow now has a hard fork — when onStage is threaded (the Issue path) the primary button is a pure local-set toggle (aria-pressed, "Stage for issue"↔"Staged"), and the only path to actions.send is mode==='send'→CockpitSendPanel, which is unreachable because nothing sets that mode on the staging branch. The header "Send issue" mutating stagedSendableIds is the single commit, with a shared-source "N staged · M already with client" counter (both off one cockpitRecs set via isCuratedForClient). B2/B9: the content plan is structurally section 3, above the proof band at section 5 (ROI/numbers collapsed behind a "See full report" user-action details toggle), the floor is exactly two honest states, and every request card carries an in-card "Let us talk" secondary button beside the request verb, both min-h-[44px], stacking flex-col on mobile, wired to open the advisor pre-seeded with the move. The scroll-to-footer warm-lead tax is gone — and the footer status line was kept, not cannibalized, so the loop surface is intact.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Blocker #5 — two-headed admin send model (no single canonical client commit)
blockerVerdicts.note: Verified in CockpitRow.tsx:139-155 and KeywordStrategy.tsx:524-542. The staging fork is real: onStage→pure useToggleSet membership toggle, no actions.send, no CockpitSendPanel opened. The send path (mode==='send') is only wired on the else branch (no onStage), so per-row send is structurally unreachable in the Issue cockpit. handleSendIssue (issueBulkSend.mutate then clear) is the lone commit; counter is shared-source (stagedSendableIds vs cockpitRecs.filter(isCuratedForClient)). canSend=stagedCount>0. This is a clean single-writer model — exactly what I asked for.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Fast-follow #9 — in-card 'let us talk' soft-yes (no scroll-to-footer warm-lead tax)
blockerVerdicts.note: Verified in IssueContentCard.tsx:196-205 and TheIssueClientPage.tsx:128-133. Each request card renders a secondary MessageCircle 'Let us talk' button beside the request verb, both min-h-[44px], flex-col sm:flex-row, wired to handleLetsTalk which opens chat and pre-seeds onAskAi with the move title+keyword. The warm-lead valve is now at the point of consideration, not 7 sections down. Footer retained (IssueLoopFooter still renders the greenlit/discussing status line), so this is additive, not a swap.

residualConcerns: LOCKED cards lose the soft-yes entirely. In IssueContentCard.tsx:182-219 the branch is isLocked ? <TierGate upsell> : <request+letsTalk pair>. So a Free client on a monetizable move sees ONLY the upsell — the in-card 'Let us talk' button vanishes for exactly the segment most likely to want a no-commitment conversation instead of a plan upgrade. The warm-lead valve I fought for is absent precisely where the lead is warmest-but-unqualified. A high-end firm wants 'let us talk' to survive the paywall; right now the paywall eats it.

The non-monetizable verb is 'Discuss this', and the monetizable card also offers 'Let us talk' — two conversation-flavored CTAs ('Let us talk' secondary + 'Discuss this' primary) can read as redundant on a non-monetizable card. Worth a quick label audit so the two soft surfaces don't compete for the same click.

previewMode silently no-ops onLetsTalk (handleLetsTalk returns early) AND keeps the button visually active/clickable — an operator previewing as client clicks 'Let us talk' and nothing happens, with no affordance that it's inert. Minor, but it undercuts the 'preview is faithful' promise.
newConcerns: The staged set is useToggleSet([], UNBOUNDED) — purely client-side, never persisted. An operator who stages 8 moves across a long curation session and then navigates away / refreshes before hitting 'Send issue' loses the entire staged selection with no warning. The single-commit model is correct, but it now has a single point of silent work-loss. A 'you have N staged, unsent' guard on navigation (or session-persisted staging) would close it. This is the cost of moving from per-row immediate-send to staged-commit — worth flagging since the old two-headed model at least committed each row as you went.
killerQuestion: Why does the in-card 'Let us talk' soft-yes — the warm-lead valve this whole fix was about — disappear behind the TierGate on locked/Free cards, when the Free-and-curious client is the single most valuable conversation a high-end firm can start? Should 'Let us talk' live OUTSIDE the locked/included fork so it survives the paywall?

lens: Agency-side positioning & sales — "is there a PRODUCT a buyer can be sold, not just a mechanism?"
round1Position: The human-curation mechanism is a real moat, but there is no product to sell yet: no external name, no headline, no positioning. The most sellable asset (the cockpit) is invisible to buyers; the client view stripped of curation-visibility looks cloneable; a thin-workspace demo lands on grey "we're evaluating" cards; and the client surface leads with a metrics/ROI bar (the dashboard frame we're trying to escape) instead of the curated POV.
round1Rating: HIGH concern — sellable mechanism, no marketable product
round2Position: Three of my four secondary demo-killers are genuinely fixed in code: the client surface now re-sequences so the content plan LEADS and ROI/numbers collapse behind a "See full report" reveal (TheIssueClientPage.tsx:179-219); the content floor is honest two-state, no "we're evaluating" filler; fabricated competitor rows are gone; and a "Curated by your strategist" byline now sits at the top making the human-curation moat visible at the point of purchase (TheIssueClientPage.tsx:173-176). That meaningfully de-risks the demo and partially answers my killer Q. BUT my two OWNED blockers — external name (#8) and the positioning layer — are untouched: D11 is explicitly deferred to fast-follow #4, "non-blocking, lock before any external demo." The code still ships zero client-facing product name. The cockpit is still invisible to buyers (no sales-deck surfacing in scope). So the mechanism is now better-demonstrated, but there is still no named, positioned PRODUCT — which is the exact thing I owned.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Fast-follow #8 — external client-facing name / positioning (the differentiator has no brand a buyer ever sees)
blockerVerdicts.note: Explicitly carved out of launch as D11 / fast-follow #4 (plan lines 137, 185, 417, 452, 921): 'OWNER (marketing). Does not block launch; lock before any external demo/sale.' Code confirms it: zero client-facing product name ships — the only 'The Issue' string in src/components/client/the-issue/ is an internal code comment (evergreenCopy.ts:3), and the evergreen guard still forbids the name reaching the client. The owner ratified this as non-blocking for an INTERNAL flag-OFF launch, which I accept as a sequencing decision — but I cannot mark my own blocker 'resolved' when the positioning layer is still empty. A differentiator you still can't name is still a feature, not a product. This is a hard gate before any external demo or website headline, and that gate has not been cleared.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Fast-follow #9 — surface the human-curation craft on the CLIENT side so the moat is visible at the moment of purchase (was: moat lives only in admin cockpit, client view looks cloneable)
blockerVerdicts.note: The 'Curated by your strategist' byline shipped (TheIssueClientPage.tsx:173-176, Sparkles + brand-accent) — that is the single cheapest fix I recommended and it's real, so the buyer now sees ONE human-curation signal. Combined with the content-plan-leads re-sequence, the client surface no longer opens on a Looker-style numbers bar. BUT it's a thin signal: a static line, no strategist name/photo, no 'reviewed and shaped' provenance, no visible evidence the system drafted-and-a-human-cut. A competitor can screenshot 'Curated by your strategist' as easily as the rest of the feed. And the actual proof-of-craft — the cockpit's drafted-POV + cut-the-sentence interaction — is STILL admin-only with no sales-deck surfacing in scope. Moat is now partially visible, not believably defended.

residualConcerns: No external product name still blocks the one thing marketing exists to do — I cannot put a headline on the website or run an external demo until D11 lands. 'Lock before any external demo' means this feature is internally launch-ready but commercially un-sellable on the day it ships.

The cockpit — my strongest SALES asset, the 'this is why we're not a $99 tool' story — remains invisible to buyers. Surfacing 2-3 cockpit screenshots in the deck was never launch scope and isn't in any fast-follow I can see, so the premium-retainer justification still lives only where prospects can't see it.

The byline is a single static string; the curation moat is asserted, not evidenced. Without a strategist identity (name/photo) or a 'drafted then human-shaped' provenance signal, the visible differentiation is still screenshot-cloneable in a month — which was the exact substance of my killer question.

No quantified proof story for marketing. The instrumentation I asked for (operator prep-time-to-ready, client engagement, greenlight rate) is still unbuilt — the outcome/export one-pager is fast-follow #3. I still have no number for a website stat or a case study.

Growth à-la-carte charge deferred to FF2 means the differentiated upsell 'moment' I'd sell is structural, not live — every paid tier sees the same un-billed 'request,' so the revenue loop I'd put in a pitch isn't actually capturing margin yet.
newConcerns: The re-sequence and byline make the demo MORE convincing while the underlying differentiation is LESS defended than the polish implies — a better-looking, cloneable-faster client surface raises the risk that a prospect (or a competitor) walks away thinking 'I could rebuild this look,' precisely because we improved the surface without yet shipping the name/provenance that would make it ours.

The 'Curated by your strategist' byline is now LIVE in code but the strategist-byline NAMING is itself listed as part of deferred D11 ('External client-facing name / strategist byline naming' — plan line 137). So we've shipped a generic placeholder byline ahead of the marketing decision that's supposed to define it — risk of a retrofit/rename later, on copy the evergreen guard has already frozen.
killerQuestion: We've improved the demo and added a 'Curated by your strategist' line — but we still ship no external product name and no strategist identity. So on the sales call where the prospect says 'this is a prettier recommendations feed I could clone in a month,' what do I actually point to — a named product? a strategist's face? a 'drafted by AI, cut by a human' provenance badge? — and if the honest answer is still 'lock the name in a fast-follow first,' how do we run a single external demo before D11 ships without conceding the exact cloneability point my round-1 killer question raised?

persona: Customer Success lead (20-50 account book), operating The Issue weekly across a portfolio
lens: Customer Success / at-scale operator throughput and client-trust safety
round1Position: Strong per-account ergonomics, but two structural gaps make it unsafe to scale: (1) no portfolio/triage layer above the single-account cockpit — POV_UNCHANGED is computed but never surfaced as a cross-account queue, so at 40 accounts I cold-open all 40 to find the 6 that changed; (2) trust-ladder auto-send fires synchronously inside the same cron tick that rings the 'ready to curate' doorbell — zero review window — so low-risk buckets are already in front of paying clients by the time I'm notified. 'Unreviewed moves shipped on a timer.'
round1Rating: matters: yes — but unsafe to scale as-built
round2Position: My higher-severity blocker (#3) is genuinely neutralized for launch — not by fixing the coupling I flagged, but by bolting an OFF-by-default flag in front of it, which is a legitimate and honest dark-launch given solo-agency scale. I credit it: the same child flag gates BOTH the cron call site (line 212) AND the operator UI (TrustLadderPanel returns null), so there is no way to enable a feature the cron won't honor, and they even added the warn-on-zero observability I'd want. But I will NOT let the digest's 'dormant, not removed' framing slide as if it were a fix: the same-tick doorbell→auto-send sequence is still in the code verbatim, so the day this flag flips on, my exact round-1 finding (no review window) returns unchanged. The resolution buys a hard launch guarantee, not a resolved design. Fast-follow #7 (portfolio triage) is exactly as absent as round 1 — POV_UNCHANGED is still per-workspace-only, no roll-up, no greenlight inbox — but that was explicitly scoped out of launch, so I judge it a deferred residual, not a launch blocker.
round2Rating: matters: yes — launch-safe for ≤5-8 accounts; the two scale gaps remain real and gate any sales push

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Blocker #3 — trust-ladder auto-send fires in the doorbell tick with zero review window (unreviewed moves shipped to paying clients on a timer)
blockerVerdicts.note: Resolved FOR LAUNCH via dark-launch, and I verified all three layers in code (not just the digest): child flag 'strategy-trust-ladder-autosend': false at feature-flags.ts:84; cron guard at strategy-issue-cron.ts:212 — runAutoSendForWorkspace is never invoked with the flag OFF; TrustLadderPanel.tsx:99 returns null and passes enabled=theIssueEnabled && autoSendEnabled so the hidden panel makes zero network calls. The single OFF flag gating BOTH the server call AND the UI is the right design — you can't flip on a feature the cron ignores. The catalog removalCondition even names the correct precondition: 'a decoupled-tick auto-send with an operator veto/review window.' And they shipped the warn-on-zero observability I implicitly wanted (strategy-issue-cron.ts ~302). Credit where due. BUT the resolution is a switch, not a fix: I confirmed the doorbell→auto-send same-tick sequence is still present verbatim in runIssuePushForWorkspaceInner (markIssuePushedWeek → addActivity/broadcast STRATEGY_ISSUE_PUSHED → guarded auto-send call), with the comment literally reading 'auto-send proceeds.' My actual finding — there is no review window — is true today as it was in round 1. It is simply unreachable by default. For launch that is sufficient and honest; the verdict is resolved because no unreviewed move can reach a client on the default path.

blockerVerdicts.blocker: Fast-follow #7 — no portfolio/triage layer; POV_UNCHANGED computed but never surfaced as a cross-account queue
blockerVerdicts.note: Unchanged from round 1, and explicitly out of launch scope (the plan sequences it as fast-follow #1, gated before onboarding past ~5-8 accounts). I verified: POV_UNCHANGED exists only as a per-workspace error string (strategy-pov-generator.ts:31) thrown and caught per-workspace in the cron and POV route — zero cross-account aggregation. No portfolio route, no roll-up component, no changedAccounts/unchangedAccounts surface anywhere in server/routes or src/components/strategy. The greenlight inbox I flagged as a related MEDIUM (dropped-greenlight risk at portfolio pace) is also still absent from the cockpit. I do not score this a launch blocker — at ≤5-8 accounts the serial slog is tolerable — but I'm not letting it pass as resolved either: it is the same gap, deferred, and it genuinely gates any multi-account rollout.

residualConcerns: The same-tick doorbell→auto-send coupling is dormant, not removed. The flag is the only thing between today's code and my original 'no review window' finding. There is no test or guard asserting that a future engineer enabling the flag ALSO ships the decoupled tick + operator-veto hold — so the safety guarantee lives entirely in tribal knowledge + a removalCondition string, which is exactly how dark-launched landmines get flipped on prematurely.

Portfolio triage (#7) and the greenlight inbox remain unbuilt, so weekly throughput across a book is still a serial cold-open of every account. The per-account 10-15 min budget is meaningless until I can see which 6 of 40 changed. This caps the product at single-operator/handful-of-accounts and must land before any sales push — which the plan acknowledges but did not build.

If the flag IS ever enabled at scale before the rework, the warn-on-zero log is observability for the WRONG failure mode — it catches 'sent zero of N candidates' (a transition error), not 'sent a bad rec the operator never saw.' There is still no operator-facing veto, hold, or 'pending auto-send' doorbell — only a server log nobody on a 40-account book is tailing.

POV-confidence/data-thinness badge (my round-1 MEDIUM for triaging which drafts need real authoring time) is not in scope here and I saw no evidence it shipped — so even within a single account, I still can't tell a rubber-stamp POV from one that needs a rewrite before I spend my 15 minutes.
newConcerns: The dark-launch introduces a latent regression surface: the auto-send code path now exists, is untested in production, and is gated only by a boolean. A flag toggle in an admin UI (the very feature-flag-toggle UI on the roadmap) could flip 'strategy-trust-ladder-autosend' on per-workspace with no accompanying review-window — re-arming my exact blocker through a config screen, not a code change. The fix made the dangerous path easier to enable than to re-architect.

The removalCondition correctly says 'enable only after a decoupled-tick auto-send with an operator veto ships,' but nothing mechanically enforces that ordering. It's a comment, not a gate. Round 1 taught us inline-duplicated predicates drift; a safety precondition that lives only in a flag-catalog string is the same class of risk.
killerQuestion: The flag is OFF today, so no client gets an unreviewed move — good. But the code that ships the move in the same tick as the doorbell is still there, untouched, with zero review window. When you (or a future operator using the feature-flag UI) flip 'strategy-trust-ladder-autosend' on for a workspace, what — in CODE, not in a removalCondition comment — forces the decoupled tick and the operator-veto hold to exist first, so the flip can't silently re-arm exactly the 'unreviewed moves on a timer' path I flagged in round 1?
